witnesses.description: A finite graph where BFS reaches the target quickly and DFS follows a long ordered tail first.
Generated shortcut-tail graphs showing finite visited-count growth.

checks.claim: BFS reaches the target through the direct shortcut after three popped vertices.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes BFS pop order and the shortest-distance map.

checks.claim: Deterministic DFS follows the ordered tail before reaching the shortcut target.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes DFS preorder and the target discovery count.

checks.claim: The finite shortcut-tail family has constant BFS visited count and growing DFS visited count for the listed sizes.
checks.notes: The validator generates each listed graph and recomputes BFS/DFS cost counters.

checks.claim: DFS reaches the target in at most three visits on the length-four shortcut-tail graph.
checks.notes: The validator rejects the bound because deterministic DFS visits six vertices before reaching the target.

checks.claim: Asymptotic BFS/DFS runtime claims require future proof resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: Asymptotic BFS/DFS complexity, graph-search lower bounds, average-case runtime, heuristic search, and parallel search
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked; asymptotic runtime theory remains a proof-assistant horizon.
